name: Nutty Tea Ring
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together 2 cups flour, sugar, salt, and yeast.
2. Heat sour cream, water, and 1/4 cup butter (or margarine) until hot to the touch; add to the dry ingredients. Mix for 2 minutes at low speed.
3. Add egg and beat for an additional 2 minutes at medium speed. Stir in enough remaining flour to form a soft dough.
4. On a floured surface, knead dough for 4 minutes.
5. Place dough in a greased bowl and turn to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 20 minutes or until doubled.
6. Punch down dough. Roll dough into a 16x9 inch rectangle.
7. Spread remaining 2 tablespoons butter evenly over the dough.
8. In a small bowl, combine dried apricots, pecans, brown sugar, and cinnamon. Sprinkle mixture over the dough.
9. Roll up from the long side as for a jelly roll, sealing the seam.
10. Place the roll seam side down on a greased baking sheet. Seal ends together to form a ring.
11. Cut slits 2/3 of the way through at 1-inch intervals.
12. Turn each section on its side and cover. Let rise in a warm place for an additional 20 minutes or until almost doubled.
13. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake the coffeecake for 20 to 25 minutes, removing from the baking sheet and cooling on a wire rack.
14. Mix milk with powdered sugar to make a glaze and drizzle over the cooled coffeecake.
